sections.label: Section 717
sections.original: σημεῖα τῆς σῆς , ὦ κάκιστʼ , ἀψυχίας .
sections.translation: A proof of thy cowardice , thou craven heart !

sections.label: Section 718
sections.original: οὔτοι πρὸς ἡμῶν γ ’ ὤλετʼ · οὐκ ἐρεῖς τόδε .
sections.translation: At any rate her death was not due to me ; this thou canst not say .

sections.label: Section 719
sections.original: φεῦ · εἴθ ’ ἀνδρὸς ἔλθοις τοῦδέ γ ’ ἐς χρείαν ποτέ .
sections.translation: Ah ! mayst thou some day come to need my aid !

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: μνήστευε πολλάς , ὡς θάνωσι πλείονες .
sections.translation: Woo many wives , that there may be the more to die .

sections.label: Section 721
sections.original: σοὶ τοῦτ ’ ὄνειδος · οὐ γὰρ ἤθελες θανεῖν .
sections.translation: That is thy reproach , for thou didst refuse to die .

sections.label: Section 722
sections.original: φίλον τὸ φέγγος τοῦτο τοῦ θεοῦ , φίλον .
sections.translation: Dear is the light of the sun-god , dear to all .

sections.label: Section 723
sections.original: κακὸν τὸ λῆμα κοὐκ ἐν ἀνδράσιν τὸ σόν .
sections.translation: A coward soul is thine , not to be reckoned among men .

sections.label: Section 724
sections.original: οὐκ ἐγγελᾷς γέροντα βαστάζων νεκρόν .
sections.translation: No laughing now for thee at bearing forth my aged corpse .

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: θανῇ γε μέντοι δυσκλεής , ὅταν θάνῃς .
sections.translation: Thy death will surely be a death of shame , come when it will .

sections.label: Section 726
sections.original: κακῶς ἀκούειν οὐ μέλει θανόντι μοι .
sections.translation: Once dead I little reck of foul report .

sections.label: Section 727
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ · τὸ γῆρας ὡς ἀναιδείας πλέων .
sections.translation: Alas ! how void of shame the old can be !

sections.label: Section 728
sections.original: ἥδ ’ οὐκ ἀναιδής · τήνδ ’ ἐφηῦρες ἄφρονα .
sections.translation: Hers was no want of shame ; ’ twas want of sense in her that thou didst find .

sections.label: Section 729
sections.original: ἄπελθε κἀμὲ τόνδ ’ ἔα θάψαι νεκρόν .
sections.translation: Begone ! and leave me to bury my dead .

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: ἄπειμι · θάψεις δ ’ αὐτὸς ὢν αὐτῆς φονεύς ,
sections.translation: I go ; bury thy victim , thyself her murderer . Her kinsmen yet will call for an account . Else surely has Acastus ceased to be a man , if he avenge not on thee his sister ’ s blood .

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: ἔρρων νυν , αὐτὸς χἡ ξυνοικήσασά σοι ,
sections.translation: Perdition seize thee and that wife of thine !

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: ἄπαιδε παιδὸς ὄντος , ὥσπερ ἄξιοι ,
sections.translation: grow old , as ye deserve , childless , though your son yet lives , for ye shall never enter the same abode with me ; nay ! were it needful I should disown thy paternal hearth by heralds ’ voice , I had disowned it . ( Exit PHERES ). Now , since we must bear our present woe ,

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: ὡς ἂν ἐν πυρᾷ θῶμεν νεκρόν .
sections.translation: let us go and lay the dead upon the pyre . [ Exit ADMETUS .

sections.label: Section 741
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰώ . σχετλία τόλμης ,
sections.translation: Woe , woe for thee ! Alas , for thy hardihood ! Noble spirit , good beyond compare , farewell ! May Hermes in the nether world , and Hades , too , give thee a kindly welcome ! and if even in that other life

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: πλέον ἔστ ’ ἀγαθοῖς , τούτων μετέχουσ ’
sections.translation: the good are rewarded , mayst thou have thy share therein and take thy seat by Hades ’ bride !

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: πολλοὺς μὲν ἤδη κἀπὸ παντοίας χθονὸς
sections.translation: Many the guests ere now from every comer of the world I have seen come to the halls of Admetus , for whom I have spread the board , but never yet

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: κακίον ’ ἐς τήνδ ’ ἑστίαν ἐδεξάμην .
sections.translation: have I welcomed to this hearth a guest so shameless as this ; a man who , in the first place , though he saw my master ’ s grief , yet entered and presumed to pass the gates , then took what cheer we had in no sober spirit , though he knew our sorrow ;

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , εἴ τι μὴ φέροιμεν , ὤτρυνεν φέρειν .
sections.translation: no ! was there aught we failed to bring ? he called for it . Next in his hands he took a goblet of ivy-wood and drank the pure juice of the black grape , till the mounting fumes of wine heated him , and he crowned his head with myrtle-sprays ,

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: ἄμουσ ’ ὑλακτῶν · δισσὰ δ ’ ἦν μέλη κλύειν ·
sections.translation: howling discordantly , while two-fold strains were there to hear , for he would sing without a thought for the troubles in Admetus ’ halls , while we servants mourned our mistress , though we did not let the stranger see our streaming eyes , for such was the bidding of Admetus .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: καὶ νῦν ἐγὼ μὲν ἐν δόμοισιν ἑστιῶ
sections.translation: So now here am I entertaining as a guest some miscreant thief maybe , or robber , while she is gone forth from the house , nor did I follow her nor stretch my hand towards her bier , in mourning for my lady , who , to me , and all her servants ,

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: μήτηρ · κακῶν γὰρ μυρίων ἐρρύετο ,
sections.translation: was a mother , for she would save us from countless trouble , appeasing her husband ’ s angry mood . Have I not good cause then to loathe this guest who cometh in our hour of woe ?

sections.label: Section 773
sections.original: οὗτος , τί σεμνὸν καὶ πεφροντικὸς βλέπεις ;
sections.translation: Ho ! sirrah , why that solemn , thoughtful look ? ’ Tis not the way for servants to scowl on guests ,

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: εἶναι , δέχεσθαι δ ’ εὐπροσηγόρῳ φρενί .
sections.translation: but with courteous soul to welcome them . But thou , seeing a friend of thy master arrive , receivest him with sullen , lowering brow , though ’ tis but a stranger that is the object of thy mourning . Come hither , that thou too mayst learn more wisdom .

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: τὰ θνητὰ πράγματ ’ οἶδας ἣν ἔχει φύσιν ;
sections.translation: Dost know the nature of this mortal state ? I trow not ; how shouldst thou ? Well , lend an ear to me . Death is the common debt of man ; no mortal really knows if he will live to see the morrow ’ s light ;

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: τὸ τῆς τύχης γὰρ ἀφανὲς οἷ προβήσεται ,
sections.translation: for Fortune ’ s issues are not in our ken , beyond the teacher ’ s rule they lie , no art can master them . Hearken then to this and learn of me , be merry , drink thy cup , and count the present day thine own , the rest to Fortune yield .

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: τίμα δὲ καὶ τὴν πλεῖστον ἡδίστην θεῶν
sections.translation: And to Cypris too , sweetest of the gods by far to man , thy tribute pay , for kindly is her mood . Let be those other cares , and heed my counsel if thou think ’ st I speak aright ; methinks I do . Come , banish this excessive grief ,

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: πίῃ μεθ ’ ἡμῶν τάσδ ’ ὑπερβαλὼν τύχας ,
sections.translation: and drink a cup with me when thou hast passed beyond these doors and wreathed thy brow ; and I feel sure the plash of wine within the cup will bring thee to a better haven from this crabbed mood , this cabined state of mind . [*] Mortals we are , and mortals ’ thoughts should have ;

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: ὡς τοῖς γε σεμνοῖς καὶ συνωφρυωμένοις
sections.translation: for all they who frown and scowl do miss ,— leastways I think so ,— the true life and get themselves misfortune .

sections.label: Section 803
sections.original: ἐπιστάμεσθα ταῦτα · νῦν δὲ πράσσομεν
sections.translation: I know all that , but our present state has little claim on revelry or laughter .

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: γυνὴ θυραῖος ἡ θανοῦσα · μὴ λίαν
sections.translation: The dead was a stranger woman ; grieve not to excess ; for the rulers of thy house are living .

sections.label: Section 807
sections.original: τί ζῶσιν ; οὐ κάτοισθα τἀν δόμοις κακά ;
sections.translation: How , living ? Thou knowest not the trouble in the house .

sections.label: Section 808
sections.original: εἰ μή τι σός με δεσπότης ἐψεύσατο .
sections.translation: I do , unless thy master did in aught deceive me .

sections.label: Section 809
sections.original: ἄγαν ἐκεῖνός ἐστ ’ ἄγαν φιλόξενος .
sections.translation: Too hospitable is he .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: οὐ χρῆν μ ’ ὀθνείου γ ’ οὕνεκ ’ εὖ πάσχειν νεκροῦ ;
sections.translation: Was I to miss good cheer because a stranger had died ?

sections.label: Section 811
sections.original: ἦ κάρτα μέντοι καὶ λίαν θυραῖος ἦν .
sections.translation: A stranger surely ! quite a stranger she !

sections.label: Section 812
sections.original: μῶν ξυμφοράν τιν ’ οὖσαν οὐκ ἔφραζέ μοι ;
sections.translation: Is there some trouble that he withheld from me ?

sections.label: Section 813
sections.original: χαίρων ἴθʼ · ἡμῖν δεσποτῶν μέλει κακά .
sections.translation: Farewell , go thy way ! my master ’ s troubles are my care .

sections.label: Section 814
sections.original: ὅδ ’ οὐ θυραίων πημάτων ἄρχει λόγος .
sections.translation: This word of thine heralds not a grief for strangers felt .

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: οὐ γάρ τι κωμάζοντ ’ ἂν ἠχθόμην σ ’ ὁρῶν .
sections.translation: Had it been , the sight of thy merriment had not grieved me so .

sections.label: Section 816
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἦ πέπονθα δείν ’ ὑπὸ ξένων ἐμῶν ;
sections.translation: Can [*] it be mine host hath strangely wronged me ?

sections.label: Section 817
sections.original: οὐκ ἦλθες ἐν δέοντι δέξασθαι δόμοις .
sections.translation: Thou earnest at no proper time for our house to welcome thee , for sorrow is come upon us ; lo ! thou seest our shorn heads and robes of sable hue .

sections.label: Section 819b
sections.original: τίς δ ’ ὁ κατθανών ;
sections.translation: Who is it that is dead ?

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: μῶν ἢ τέκνων τι φροῦδον ἢ γέρων πατήρ ;
sections.translation: Is it a child or his aged sire that hat hath passed away ?

sections.label: Section 821
sections.original: γυνὴ μὲν οὖν ὄλωλεν Ἀδμήτου , ξένε .
sections.translation: Nay , sir guest , ’ tis Admetus ’ wife that is no more .

sections.label: Section 822
sections.original: τί φῄς ; ἔπειτα δῆτά μ ’ ἐξενίζετε ;
sections.translation: What sayest thou ? and did ye then in spite of that admit me to your cheer ?

sections.label: Section 823
sections.original: ᾐδεῖτο γάρ σε τῶνδ ’ ἀπώσασθαι δόμων .
sections.translation: Yes , for his regard would not let him send thee from his door .

sections.label: Section 824
sections.original: ὦ σχέτλιʼ , οἵας ἤμπλακες ξυναόρου .
sections.translation: Unhappy husband , what a wife hast thou lost !

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: ἀπωλόμεσθα πάντες , οὐ κείνη μόνη .
sections.translation: We are all undone , not she alone .

sections.label: Section 826
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ᾐσθόμην μὲν ὄμμ ’ ἰδὼν δακρυρροοῦν
sections.translation: I knew it when I saw his streaming eye , shorn head and downcast look , yet did he persuade me , saying it was a stranger he was bearing to burial . So I did constrain myself and passed his gates

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: ἔπινον ἀνδρὸς ἐν φιλοξένου δόμοις ,
sections.translation: and sat drinking in his hospitable halls , when he was suffering thus . And have I wreathed my head and do I revel still ? But — thou to hold thy peace when such a crushing sorrow lay upon the house ! Where is he burying her ? Whither shall I go to find her ?

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: ὀρθὴν παρ ’ οἶμον , ἣ ’ πὶ Λάρισαν φέρει ,
sections.translation: Beside the road that leadeth straight to Larissa , shalt thou see her carved tomb outside the suburb .

sections.label: Section 837
sections.original: ὦ πολλὰ τλᾶσα καρδία καὶ χεὶρ ἐμή ,
sections.translation: O heart , O soul , both sufferers oft , now show the mettle of that son Tirynthian Alcmena , daughter of Electryon , bare to Zeus .

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: δεῖ γάρ με σῶσαι τὴν θανοῦσαν ἀρτίως
sections.translation: For I must save this woman , dead but now , setting Alcestis once again within this house , and to Admetus this kind service render . So I will go and watch for death the black-robed monarch of the dead , and him methinks I shall find

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: πίνοντα τύμβου πλησίον προσφαγμάτων .
sections.translation: as he drinks of the blood-offering near the tomb . And if , from ambush rushing , once I catch and fold him in my arms ’ embrace , none shall ever wrest him thence with smarting ribs , ere he give up the woman unto me .

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: ἢν δ ’ οὖν ἁμάρτω τῆσδ ’ ἄγρας , καὶ μὴ μόλῃ
sections.translation: But should I fail to find my prey and he come not to the clotted blood , I will go to the sunless home of those beneath the earth , to Persephone and her king , and make to them my prayer , sure that I shall bring Alcestis up again , to place her in the hands of him , my host ,

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: ὅς μ ’ ἐς δόμους ἐδέξατ ’ οὐδ ’ ἀπήλασε ,
sections.translation: who welcomed me to his house nor drove me thence , though fortune smote him hard , but this his noble spirit strove to hide out of regard for me . What host more kind than him in Thessaly ? or in in the homes of Hellas ? Wherefore shall he never say
